Have a small free bet riding on Holloway that I gave on my free podcast but for official bets we are small on some totals and props. So many variables in this one, can’t go big on it at all. Discipline is important but I do lean Holloway and fight to end in a stoppage either way.

Very curious to see if Topuria destroyed Holloway’s chin. All time chin before that but so often once a strong chin gets cracked it turns to glass. Conor couldn’t hurt Holloway with clean bombs in their first fight. If he hurts him here it’s because Max’s chin is cracked.
BSD is a perennial flake. So many underperformances even in wins. Verifiably unreliable from this point on.

wOBA by month to strip out noise - conclusions hold directionally

June 2026:
• Highest single-month wOBA since 2019
• Largest month-over-month wOBA gain of the Statcast era (+.018, ~3.4x a normal month)

Would genuinely value @tangotiger's read: is there an explanation for the sudden drag drop / carry spike that isn't the ball? Hard to think of anyone better to weigh in

On Memorial Day, barrel carry jumped ~9 feet overnight and hasn't come down - erasing five years of a deadened ball in a single week

The Data Behind MLB's Offensive Surge in June:

https://t.co/pK8uWOyFkb

4.77 in June = highest mark in a full season month since 2019 (juiced ball)

Also, the highest month-over-month gain in the statcast era (+0.47 runs) https://t.co/bDlCrny4j6

Opening Pitch for Monday, June 22

https://t.co/9QqMdYQfjt

I have controlled for the weather (indoors and outdoors) and will present all of my research tomorrow

Better weather can explain about 40-50% of the increase, at most

Something else undeniably changed https://t.co/GURnXBLv7C

Example One of Many

Eli White, 4/25, 103.0/21°, 370 ft flyout (Temp: 73; Wind: 5 mph out LCF)
vs.
Mauricio Dubón, 6/3, 103.2/22°, 405 ft HR (+35 ft; Temp: 75; Wind: 9 mph in from LCF) https://t.co/hveTcpGxPN

There is strong evidence that MLB either changed the baseballs or introduced a new batch of baseballs (while still cycling out the old) in late May / early June 

Almost every contact-quality-controlled Statcast metric has seen an outlier uptick in recent weeks

Barrel distance increased by +10.2 ft from April 2026 to present, the biggest in-season April to June jump of the Statcast era (avg is +4.6 ft as weather improves)

The additional ~6 ft this June aligns with the ~0.016 point drop in the drag data

Compiling additional findings 💉⚾️🚨

Rico Verhoeven was NOT robbed of a century level upset. Sure, the referee may have jumped the gun. However, we would have witnessed a completely different bout in Usyk vs. Rico had it not been for open scoring. Make no mistake about it.
The @WBCBoxing  utilized an open scoring system for this fight, meaning the judges' scorecards were announced between rounds. After eight rounds, the fight was tied 76-76 on all three cards. This allowed both corners to adjust their strategies in real-time. But let's look at the bigger picture: Usyk @usykboxing  underwhelming pace was a calculated move. He could have gotten him out of there much earlier.

Instead, pacing the fight builds the drama necessary to set up a 1/2 dozen more lucrative, cross sport paydays. This is the reality of the modern crossover promotion machine. We have seen this playbook run time & time again:
🥊 Mayweather vs. McGregor: @TheNotoriousMMA  was gifted the first 3 rounds by the defensive GOAT. Floyd opened as a massive -2500 favorite but closed at just -400 because the public bought into the manufactured hype. @FloydMayweather  wasted no time overseas with  Tenshin Nasukawa.
🥊 Fury vs. Ngannou: Tyson @Tyson_Fury never stepped on the gas, got knocked down, and entirely changed the fight dynamic resulting in a Draw & another massive payday for Francis. 
🥊 Joshua vs. Ngannou & Paul: AJ wasn't going to make Fury's mistake w/ @francis_ngannou, getting him outa there exactly like he was supposed to. (Ngannou down in round one and twice in round two.) Even against Jake Paul, Joshua coasted until he decided to step on the gas late, and when he did, Jake’s jaw ended up needing to be wired.

These fighters are -2500 favorites for a reason. The skill disparity in these crossover fights is absolutely insurmountable, especially when you are talking about the top 3 Boxing heavyweights of their era.
This cycle will keep happening. It’s a rinse & repeat model for massive paydays, & from a business perspective, that’s great for everyone involved.

But when it comes to wagering, if you like money, you can't buy into underdog hype or rely on results from past crossover spectacles. Bettors often fall into trap of thinking, "Well, the underdog can win 1 in 20 times at those odds so lets take a shot."
The problem is "they don't fight 20 times. They fight once." 🍿🥯👊
